title: Intensely Salty British Clams Pasta
description: A show-off British dish featuring clams, pasta, and a creamy emulsion, made in a copper pot and paleo-friendly, with a salty flavor profile and pillowy texture.
instructions: Bring a large copper pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pappardelle pasta according to the package instructions until al dente.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining the pasta and set it aside.
In the same copper pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the minced garlic until fragrant.
Add the freshly shucked clams to the pot and cook until they open, about 3-5 minutes.
Remove the clams from the pot and set them aside, leaving the garlic and oil mixture behind.
Add the reserved pasta water to the pot and stir to combine with the garlic and oil mixture, bringing the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 2 minutes, allowing it to emulsify and thicken.
Stir in the sea salt, black pepper, and lemon juice, and cook for an additional minute.
Add the cooked pappardelle pasta to the pot and toss to combine with the emulsion sauce.
Return the cooked clams to the pot and toss to combine with the pasta and sauce.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve immediately.
